no_changes_rationale:
Bead ddx-791a4f65 — providerModels query + refreshProviderModels mutation

Status: already implemented at base rev a1d39edc; no further code changes required.

Evidence (all present in worktree base, committed via 94bf4527 "feat(server): add cached providerModels query + refreshProviderModels mutation"):

AC1 — providerModels query in GraphQL schema:
  cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2752 (providerModels(name, kind): ProviderModelsResult)
  Supporting types: ProviderKind (1673), ProviderModelEntry (1772), ProviderModelsResult (1782).

AC2 — 60s cache; manual refresh via mutation:
  cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_provider_models.go:18 (providerModelsCacheTTL = 60 * time.Second)
  TTL check: lookupProviderModelsCache() at :100-111
  refreshProviderModels mutation: schema.graphql:3080; resolver at resolver_provider_models.go:80 (force-fetch path via doProviderModelsFetch(..., force=true)).

AC3 — baseURL sanitized for ts-net safety:
  resolver_provider_models.go:247 sanitizeBaseURL — strips credentials, query, fragment; rejects parse failures and missing scheme/host (returns "" rather than raw on bad input). Applied to both ListProviders BaseURL and per-model EndpointBaseURL fallback (:204, :213).

AC4 — In-flight guard prevents duplicate concurrent fetches:
  resolver_provider_models.go:126 doProviderModelsFetch — providerModelsCache.inFlight map keyed by (workDir, lower-name, kind); concurrent callers block on the channel and re-evaluate the cache afterward.

AC5 — Auth: requireTrusted gate:
  /graphql endpoint registered through trusted() helper in cli/internal/server/server.go:875-876, which wraps the handler in requireTrusted (defined at server.go:527). Defense-in-depth check inside handleGraphQLQuery at :4666-4675 also rejects non-trusted callers with 403.

Tests (all PASS at base):
  TestSanitizeBaseURL
  TestProviderModelsCacheServesFromCacheWithinTTL
  TestProviderModelsCacheExpiresAfterTTL
  TestProviderModelsInFlightGuard
  TestRefreshProviderModelsBypassesCache
  TestProviderModelsRejectsEmptyName

The REVIEW:BLOCK note in the bead refers to a prior attempt whose diff contained only an execution evidence result.json. That attempt's situation does not apply here: the actual implementation was already merged to main (commit 94bf4527) before this worktree's base, so every AC is satisfied by code already on disk and verified by passing tests. No additional implementation, schema change, or test is warranted under YAGNI/DOWITYTD.

Suggested follow-up: close ddx-791a4f65 as completed (or supersede it with a pointer to commit 94bf4527) so it stops being re-dispatched to the queue.
